report.executive_summary:
TECHNICAL UNDERWRITING REPORT – CLOSING READINESS: 67/100

This transaction presents a CRITICAL blocking issue that must be resolved before closing can proceed. The recorded warranty deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees, directly contradicting Requirement 4, which mandates conveyance to proposed buyer Kyla B. Fortenberry. This grantee discrepancy creates an absolute title defect that prevents issuance of the owner's policy and must be corrected through immediate re-recording of a corrected deed.

Additionally, a MEDIUM-priority concern exists regarding the vested owner/seller discrepancy: Garner Tree Farm, LLC holds title, but Terri G. Crawley is listed as seller. This requires immediate clarification and LLC authorization documentation (operating agreement, member resolution, good standing certificate) per Requirement 6.

A HIGH-priority technical issue involves duplicate and overlapping exceptions for mineral reservations (Exceptions 1 and 8) that require consolidation and clarification in the final policy. The warranty deed confirms minerals are severed from the property ("LESS AND EXCEPT all oil, gas, and other minerals"), but the dual exception language creates ambiguity.

Once the deed is corrected to name Kyla B. Fortenberry as grantee and all LLC compliance documents are submitted, the remaining requirements (deed of trust recording and final verifications) are routine and should clear quickly. The property has no tax delinquencies, and standard easements are already noted as exceptions. This file is positioned for closing once the critical grantee discrepancy is resolved.

report.requirements:
REQUIREMENT 1 – VESTED OWNER/SELLER DISCREPANCY [OPEN – MEDIUM PRIORITY]
Requirement: Advise customer service of circumstances explaining discrepancy between vested owner (Garner Tree Farm, LLC) and seller (Terri G. Crawley)
Status: NOT SATISFIED
Analysis: The commitment identifies Garner Tree Farm, LLC as the vested owner holding recorded title. However, Terri G. Crawley is listed as the seller. Terri Garner Crawley is identified as a member of the LLC. This discrepancy must be explained and documented. The LLC likely authorized Terri G. Crawley to act as seller on behalf of the LLC, but this authorization must be evidenced through proper LLC documentation.
Action Required: Submit written explanation from seller/LLC regarding authorization. Coordinate with Requirement 6 (LLC documentation).

REQUIREMENT 2 – VERIFY OPEN DEED OF TRUST [OPEN – LOW PRIORITY]
Requirement: No open deed of trust found in Marion County records; verify by inquiry of escrow personnel and/or proposed insured
Status: PENDING VERIFICATION
Analysis: Title search reveals no open deed of trust encumbering the property in Marion County records. This is a routine verification requirement to confirm no outstanding liens exist that would need to be satisfied at closing.
Action Required: Obtain written confirmation from escrow personnel and/or proposed insured that no other deeds of trust exist. This is routine and should clear quickly once other critical issues are resolved.

REQUIREMENT 3 – BANKRUPTCY SEARCH [SATISFIED]
Requirement: No bankruptcy proceedings found for Garner Tree Farm, LLC, Terri G. Crawley, and Kyla B. Fortenberry
Status: SATISFIED
Analysis: Bankruptcy search has been completed with negative results for all three parties. No bankruptcy filings exist that would affect title or the transaction.

REQUIREMENT 4 – RECORD DEED TO PROPER GRANTEE [OPEN – CRITICAL BLOCKING ISSUE]
Requirement: Record satisfactory executed Deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC, grantor(s) to Kyla B. Fortenberry, grantee(s), conveying the subject premises in Marion County records
Status: NOT SATISFIED – CRITICAL DEFECT
Analysis: This is the most critical issue in the file. A deed was recorded on 01/13/2026 (Instrument #20260174) from Garner Tree Farm, LLC as grantor. However, the grantees named in this deed are Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson—NOT Kyla B. Fortenberry as required by this commitment requirement. This represents a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates an absolute title defect that prevents issuance of the owner's policy to Kyla B. Fortenberry.

The recorded deed conveys title to the wrong parties. A corrected deed must be executed and recorded immediately, naming Kyla B. Fortenberry as the sole grantee. This is a blocking issue that cannot be waived and must be resolved before closing.

Action Required: IMMEDIATE – Obtain corrected warranty deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry. Execute and record in Marion County records. Provide recorded copy to underwriting for verification before policy issuance.

REQUIREMENT 5 – RECORD DEED OF TRUST [OPEN – ROUTINE]
Requirement: Record satisfactory executed Deed of Trust from Kyla B. Fortenberry as Trustor, in favor of Society Mortgage, as Beneficiary, securing an original principal balance of $80,808.00
Status: PENDING
Analysis: This is a routine requirement contingent upon Requirement 4 being satisfied. Once title is properly conveyed to Kyla B. Fortenberry, the deed of trust can be executed and recorded. This requirement should clear quickly once the grantee discrepancy is resolved.
Action Required: Upon satisfaction of Requirement 4, execute and record deed of trust in Marion County records. Provide recorded copy to underwriting.

REQUIREMENT 6 – LLC DOCUMENTATION AND COMPLIANCE [OPEN – HIGH PRIORITY]
Requirement: Submit: (a) Copy of operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) Evidence LLC is in good standing; (c) Resolution signed by all members authorizing transaction; (d) Other requirements following review of organizational documents
Status: NOT SATISFIED
Analysis: Garner Tree Farm, LLC is a Mississippi limited liability company with four identified members: Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, and Karen Garner Delancey. Before the LLC can execute and deliver a corrected deed to Kyla B. Fortenberry, the LLC must provide evidence of proper authorization and good standing. This includes:
  • Operating agreement and any amendments (or certification that none exist)
  • Certificate of good standing from Mississippi Secretary of State
  • Member resolution signed by all four members authorizing the sale and conveyance to Kyla B. Fortenberry
  • Any other organizational documents required upon review

This requirement is essential to confirm the LLC has authority to convey title and that all members have authorized the transaction.

Action Required: Obtain and submit all LLC organizational documents, good standing certificate, and member authorization resolution. This must be completed before the corrected deed can be recorded.
